title: Aggregate-vs-individual-data signal extractor
context: Reusers of the corpus need to know, per article, whether the reported data is aggregate or individual-level, and whether the article mentions controlled-access data sources. This signal is extracted from data-availability statements (and, where licenses permit derivative processing, the full text). CC-BY-ND articles must be processed for facts only — no derivative full-text redistribution. The signal populates dataAvailability {aggregateOnly, mentionsControlledAccess, datasetRefs[]} on the canonical record. Depends on fullbuild-010 and gate-002 (for the ND rule confirmation).
objective: Implement the aggregate-vs-individual-data signal extractor that parses data-availability statements (and, where permitted, full text) to populate dataAvailability {aggregateOnly, mentionsControlledAccess, datasetRefs[]} on the canonical record, with ND-licensed articles processed for facts only, and spot-audited for correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Parses data-availability statements (and, where the article license permits derivative processing, full text) to set aggregateOnly:boolean, mentionsControlledAccess:boolean, and datasetRefs[] on every applicable record.

CC-BY-ND and CC-BY-NC-ND licensed articles are processed for descriptive facts only; no derivative full-text transformation is applied to them; this ND rule is documented and confirmed by the License+compliance reviewer.
COSMIC/OncoKB references in datasetRefs[] are flagged (non-commercial/custom terms); TCGA-GDC-open/GEO-open references are permissible and labeled as open; this mapping follows gate-002.
A spot-audit sample of ≥ 50 articles is reviewed for correctness; audit results (correct/incorrect counts, any error patterns) are recorded and committed.
Code MIT licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: pmc-cancer-fullbuild-010 (article pool and full-text availability flags)
pmc-cancer-gate-002 (ND rule — facts yes, derivative full text no; COSMIC/OncoKB flag rule)
PLAN.md (Data, licensing & compliance — Referenced external datasets; Open questions — ND fact extraction)
output: A merged PR adding the data-availability signal extractor module (TypeScript/ESM), documentation of the ND processing rule (reviewer-confirmed), spot-audit results committed, and CI tests covering the controlled-access detection and ND safeguard.
